web_text: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are in the Dispersion: Greetings. Count it all joy, my brothers, when you fall into various temptations, knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance. Let endurance have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.
slang_text: It's James here, a servant of God and the Lord Jesus Christ, writin' to the twelve tribes scattered all over the place: Greetin's. Count it as pure joy, my brothers, whenever you fall into all kinds of trials, knowin' that testin' your faith builds up endurance. Let that endurance finish its job all the way, so you turn out complete, missin' nothin'.

web_text: But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach; and it will be given to him. But let him ask in faith, without any doubting, for he who doubts is like a wave of the sea, driven by the wind and tossed. For that man shouldn’t think that he will receive anything from the Lord. He is a double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.
slang_text: But if any of you is comin' up short on wisdom, ask God for it — He hands it out generous to everybody, no judgin' involved, and you'll get it. But ask with faith, no second-guessin', 'cause whoever doubts is like a wave out on the ocean, blown around and tossed by the wind. That guy shouldn't figure he's gonna get anything from the Lord. He's double-minded, unstable in every single thing he does.

web_text: But let the brother in humble circumstances glory in his high position; and the rich, in that he is made humble, because like the flower in the grass, he will pass away. For the sun arises with the scorching wind, and withers the grass, and the flower in it falls, and the beauty of its appearance perishes. So also will the rich man fade away in his pursuits. Blessed is the man who endures temptation, for when he has been approved, he will receive the crown of life, which the Lord promised to those who love him.
slang_text: But let the brother who's broke brag about his high standin', and let the rich guy brag about gettin' humbled, 'cause like a flower in the grass, he's gonna fade away. The sun rises with a scorchin' wind, withers the grass, the flower drops off, and its good looks disappear — same deal, the rich guy's gonna fade away right in the middle of chasin' his stuff. Blessed is the guy who hangs tough through temptation, 'cause once he's proven himself, he's gonna score the crown of life, the one the Lord promised everybody who loves Him.

web_text: Let no man say when he is tempted, “I am tempted by God,” for God can’t be tempted by evil, and he himself tempts no one. But each one is tempted when he is drawn away by his own lust, and enticed. Then the lust, when it has conceived, bears sin; and the sin, when it is full grown, produces death. Don’t be deceived, my beloved brothers.
slang_text: Don't let nobody say, when they're gettin' tempted, "God's temptin' me" — 'cause God can't get tempted by evil, and He never tempts nobody Himself. Everybody gets tempted when their own cravings drag 'em off and hook 'em. Then that craving, once it's cooked, gives birth to sin, and that sin, once it's full grown, cranks out death. Don't get conned, my beloved brothers.

web_text: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom can be no variation, nor turning shadow. Of his own will he gave birth to us by the word of truth, that we should be a kind of first fruits of his creatures. So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger; for the anger of man doesn’t produce the righteousness of God.
slang_text: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes down from above, straight from the Father of lights, who never changes up or casts shiftin' shadows. He birthed us on His own will through the word of truth, so we'd end up like the first fruits of everything He made. So, my beloved brothers, everybody oughta be quick to listen, slow to talk, slow to get mad — 'cause a person's anger doesn't produce God's righteousness, no way.

web_text: Therefore, putting away all filthiness and overflowing of wickedness, receive with humility the implanted word, which is able to save your souls. But be doers of the word, and not only hearers, deluding your own selves. For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man looking at his natural face in a mirror; for he sees himself, and goes away, and immediately forgets what kind of man he was.
slang_text: So ditch all the grody filth and overflowin' wickedness, and take in, humble-like, the word that's already planted in you, which can save your souls. But be doers of the word, not just listeners, conning yourselves. 'Cause if somebody just listens to the word without doin' nothin' about it, he's like a guy checkin' out his own face in a mirror — he looks, walks away, and forgets what he even looked like right away.

web_text: But he who looks into the perfect law of freedom, and continues, not being a hearer who forgets, but a doer of the work, this man will be blessed in what he does. If anyone among you thinks himself to be religious while he doesn’t bridle his tongue, but deceives his heart, this man’s religion is worthless. Pure religion and undefiled before our God and Father is this: to visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep oneself unstained by the world.
slang_text: But whoever looks into the perfect law of freedom and sticks with it, not just listenin' and forgettin', but actually doin' the work — that guy's gonna be blessed in whatever he does. If anybody thinks he's religious while he can't keep his own mouth in check, conning his own heart, his religion's worthless. Pure, spotless religion in front of our God and Father is this: lookin' after orphans and widows in their hard times, and keepin' yourself from gettin' stained up by this world.

web_text: My brothers, don’t hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ of glory with partiality. For if a man with a gold ring, in fine clothing, comes into your synagogue, and a poor man in filthy clothing also comes in; and you pay special attention to him who wears the fine clothing, and say, “Sit here in a good place”; and you tell the poor man, “Stand there,” or “Sit by my footstool”; haven’t you shown partiality among yourselves, and become judges with evil thoughts?
slang_text: My brothers, don't hold onto the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ of glory while playin' favorites. 'Cause if some dude rolls up in a gold ring and fancy threads, and a poor guy in raggedy clothes shows up too, and you fawn all over the fancy dude, sayin', "Sit right here in the good seat," but tell the poor guy, "Stand over there," or "Sit down by my feet" — haven't you just shown favoritism among yourselves, turnin' into judges with evil thoughts?

web_text: Listen, my beloved brothers. Didn’t God choose those who are poor in this world to be rich in faith, and heirs of the Kingdom which he promised to those who love him? But you have dishonored the poor man. Don’t the rich oppress you, and personally drag you before the courts? Don’t they blaspheme the honorable name by which you are called? However, if you fulfill the royal law, according to the Scripture, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself,” you do well.
slang_text: Listen up, my beloved brothers. Didn't God pick out the folks who're poor in this world to be rich in faith, heirs of the Kingdom He promised to everybody who loves Him? But you dissed the poor guy. Aren't the rich the ones oppressin' you, draggin' you into court personally? Don't they blaspheme the honorable name you're called by? But if you keep the royal law, straight outta Scripture — "Love your neighbor like you love yourself" — you're doin' just fine.

web_text: But if you show partiality, you commit sin, being convicted by the law as transgressors. For whoever keeps the whole law, and yet stumbles in one point, he has become guilty of all. For he who said, “Do not commit adultery,” also said, “Do not commit murder.” Now if you do not commit adultery, but murder, you have become a transgressor of the law. So speak, and so do, as men who are to be judged by a law of freedom.
slang_text: But if you play favorites, you're sinnin', flat-out convicted by the law as a lawbreaker. 'Cause whoever keeps the whole law but trips up on one point's already guilty of the whole thing. The same guy who said, "Don't cheat on your spouse," also said, "Don't murder nobody." So if you don't cheat but you murder, you've still broken the law. So talk and act like folks who're gonna get judged by a law of freedom.

web_text: For judgment is without mercy to him who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment. What good is it, my brothers, if a man says he has faith, but has no works? Can faith save him? And if a brother or sister is naked and in lack of daily food, and one of you tells them, “Go in peace, be warmed and filled”; and yet you didn’t give them the things the body needs, what good is it?
slang_text: 'Cause judgment shows no mercy to whoever showed no mercy. Mercy wins out over judgment, every time. What good is it, my brothers, if some guy says he's got faith but he's got no deeds to back it up? Can faith by itself save him? And if a brother or sister's got nothin' to wear and no food for the day, and one of you says, "Go in peace, stay warm, eat well" — but you don't actually give 'em what their body needs, what good's that do?

web_text: Even so faith, if it has no works, is dead in itself. Yes, a man will say, “You have faith, and I have works.” Show me your faith without works, and I by my works will show you my faith. You believe that God is one. You do well. The demons also believe, and shudder. But do you want to know, vain man, that faith apart from works is dead?
slang_text: Same deal — faith without any deeds is dead, plain and simple. Yeah, somebody's gonna say, "You got faith, and I got deeds." Show me your faith without deeds, and I'll show you my faith through my deeds. You believe there's one God? Cool, good for you. The demons believe that too, and they shake in their boots over it. But you wanna know somethin', dipstick — faith without deeds is dead?

web_text: Wasn’t Abraham our father justified by works, in that he offered up Isaac his son on the altar? You see that faith worked with his works, and by works faith was perfected; and the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him as righteousness”; and he was called the friend of God. You see then that by works, a man is justified, and not only by faith.
slang_text: Wasn't Abraham our father made righteous through his deeds, offerin' up his son Isaac on the altar? You see how faith worked hand in hand with his deeds, and how his deeds made his faith complete — and that fulfilled the Scripture that says, "Abraham believed God, and it got counted to him as righteousness," and he got called God's friend. So you can see it's through deeds a person's made righteous, not through faith alone.

web_text: In the same way, wasn’t Rahab the prostitute also justified by works, in that she received the messengers, and sent them out another way? For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.
slang_text: Same deal with Rahab the prostitute — wasn't she made righteous through her deeds too, takin' in the messengers and sneakin' 'em out a back way? 'Cause just like a body without a spirit is dead, faith without deeds is dead too.

web_text: Let not many of you be teachers, my brothers, knowing that we will receive heavier judgment. For in many things we all stumble. If anyone doesn’t stumble in word, the same is a perfect man, able to bridle the whole body also. Indeed, we put bits into the horses’ mouths so that they may obey us, and we guide their whole body. Behold, the ships also, though they are so big and are driven by fierce winds, are yet guided by a very small rudder, wherever the pilot desires.
slang_text: Don't let too many of you jump to be teachers, my brothers, knowin' we're gonna get judged even harder for it. We all trip up in a ton of ways. If somebody never trips up in what he says, that guy's basically perfect, able to keep his whole body in check too. Check it out, we stick bits in horses' mouths so they obey us, and that steers their whole body. And ships — huge as they are, blown around by wild winds — still get steered by a tiny little rudder, wherever the captain wants to go.

web_text: So the tongue is also a little member, and boasts great things. See how a small fire can spread to a large forest! And the tongue is a fire. The world of iniquity among our members is the tongue, which defiles the whole body, and sets on fire the course of nature, and is set on fire by Gehenna. For every kind of animal, bird, creeping thing, and sea creature, is tamed, and has been tamed by mankind; but nobody can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
slang_text: Same deal with the tongue — it's a small little thing, but it brags about huge stuff. Check out how one little spark can torch a whole forest! The tongue's a fire too. It's the whole world of wickedness stuffed into our own bodies, dirtyin' up the whole system, settin' fire to your entire life, and it's lit itself by the fires of hell. Every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and sea creature's gotten tamed, tamed by humans — but nobody's ever tamed the tongue. It's restless, evil, loaded with deadly poison.

web_text: With it we bless our God and Father, and with it we curse men, who are made in the image of God. Out of the same mouth comes blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things ought not to be so. Does a spring send out from the same opening fresh and bitter water? Can a fig tree, my brothers, yield olives, or a vine figs? Thus no spring yields both salt water and fresh water.
slang_text: We use it to bless God our Father, and we turn right around and curse people made in God's own image with it. Blessin' and cursin' comin' outta the exact same mouth. My brothers, that's just not how it oughta be. Does a spring shoot out fresh water and bitter water from the same hole? Can a fig tree grow olives, my brothers, or a grapevine grow figs? No spring produces salt water and fresh water both.

web_text: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by his good conduct that his deeds are done in gentleness of wisdom. But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, don’t boast and don’t lie against the truth. This wisdom is not that which comes down from above, but is earthly, sensual, and demonic. For where jealousy and selfish ambition are, there is confusion and every evil deed.
slang_text: Who's wise and sharp among you all? Let him prove it by livin' right, doin' his deeds with gentle wisdom. But if you've got bitter jealousy and selfish ambition eatin' at your heart, don't go braggin' and lyin' against the truth. That kinda wisdom don't come down from above — it's earthly, fleshly, straight-up demonic. 'Cause wherever jealousy and selfish ambition are runnin' the show, there's chaos and every kinda evil deed you can think of.

web_text: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy. Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
slang_text: But the wisdom that comes down from above's pure first of all, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable, loaded with mercy and good fruit, no favoritism, no hypocrisy. And the fruit of righteousness gets planted in peace by the ones who make peace happen.

web_text: Where do wars and fightings among you come from? Don’t they come from your pleasures that war in your members? You lust, and don’t have. You murder and covet, and can’t obtain. You fight and make war. You don’t have, because you don’t ask. You ask, and don’t receive, because you ask with wrong motives, so that you may spend it on your pleasures. You adulterers and adulteresses, don’t you know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
slang_text: Where's all this fightin' and squabblin' among you comin' from? Ain't it from your own cravings warrin' inside you? You want stuff and don't get it. You kill and covet and still can't score it. You fight, you go to war. You don't got it 'cause you don't ask. You ask and don't get it 'cause you're askin' for the wrong reasons, just to blow it on your own pleasures. You cheaters, don't you know bein' buddies with this world means bein' at war with God? So whoever wants to be the world's friend is makin' himself God's enemy.

web_text: Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, “The Spirit who lives in us yearns jealously”? But he gives more grace. Therefore it says, “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.” Be subject therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners; and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
slang_text: Or you think Scripture's just talkin' for no reason when it says, "The Spirit livin' in us wants us jealously, all to Himself"? But He hands out more grace. That's why it says, "God fights the proud, but pours out grace on the humble." So yield to God. Fight back against the devil, and he'll bolt from you. Get close to God, and He'll get close to you. Clean up your hands, you sinners; purify your hearts, you double-minded fence-sitters.

web_text: Lament, mourn, and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning, and your joy to gloom. Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he will exalt you. Don’t speak against one another, brothers. He who speaks against a brother and judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law, but a judge. Only one is the lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge another?
slang_text: Grieve, mourn, cry your eyes out. Let your laughter flip into mournin', your joy into gloom. Humble yourselves in front of the Lord, and He's gonna lift you up. Don't talk trash on each other, brothers. Whoever trash-talks a brother, judgin' him, is trash-talkin' the law, judgin' the law itself. But if you're judgin' the law, you're not livin' it out, you're just playin' judge. There's only one lawgiver, and He's the one who can save or destroy. So who are you to judge somebody else?

web_text: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow let’s go into this city, and spend a year there, trade, and make a profit.” Whereas you don’t know what your life will be like tomorrow. For what is your life? For you are a vapor, that appears for a little time, and then vanishes away. For you ought to say, “If the Lord wills, we will both live, and do this or that.” But now you glory in your boasting. All such boasting is evil.
slang_text: Now listen up, you guys sayin', "Today or tomorrow we're headin' to this city, spendin' a year there, tradin' and rakin' in the profit." You don't even know what your life's gonna look like tomorrow. What even is your life, anyway? You're a puff of smoke, showin' up for a hot minute and then just vanishin'. What you oughta say instead is, "If the Lord's willin', we'll live and do this or that." But instead you're braggin' like you're all that. Braggin' like that's just bogus.

web_text: To him therefore who knows to do good, and doesn’t do it, to him it is sin.
slang_text: So whoever knows the good thing to do and doesn't do it — that's sin, plain and simple.

web_text: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming on you. Your riches are corrupted and your garments are moth-eaten. Your gold and your silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be for a testimony against you, and will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up your treasure in the last days. Behold, the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you have kept back by fraud, cry out, and the cries of those who reaped have entered into the ears of the Lord of Armies.
slang_text: Listen up, you rich folks — cry and wail over the misery that's headed your way. Your riches have gone rotten and your clothes are moth-eaten. Your gold and silver have corroded, and that corrosion's gonna testify against you, eatin' your flesh like fire. You've been stackin' up treasure right in the last days. Check it out — the wages you cheated outta the workers who harvested your fields are cryin' out, and the shouts of them harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of Armies.

web_text: You have lived in luxury on the earth, and taken your pleasure. You have nourished your hearts as in a day of slaughter. You have condemned, you have murdered the righteous one. He doesn’t resist you. Be patient therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient over it, until it receives the early and late rain. You also be patient. Establish your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.
slang_text: You've lived it up in luxury down here, chasin' your own pleasure. You fattened yourselves up like it's a day of slaughter. You condemned and murdered the righteous guy, and he didn't fight you on it. So be patient, brothers, till the Lord comes back. Check out the farmer, waitin' on the precious harvest from the ground, stayin' patient till it gets the early rain and the late rain. You be patient too. Lock your hearts in solid, 'cause the Lord's comin' back real soon.

web_text: Don’t grumble, brothers, against one another, so that you won’t be judged. Behold, the judge stands at the door. Take, brothers, for an example of suffering and of perseverance, the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord. Behold, we call them blessed who endured. You have heard of the perseverance of Job, and have seen the Lord in the outcome, and how the Lord is full of compassion and mercy. But above all things, my brothers, don’t swear— not by heaven, or by the earth, or by any other oath; but let your “yes” be “yes”, and your “no”, “no”; so that you don’t fall into hypocrisy.
slang_text: Don't grumble against each other, brothers, so you don't get judged. Check it out, the judge is already standin' right at the door. Take the prophets who spoke in the Lord's name as your example for sufferin' and stickin' it out, brothers. We call the ones who endured, blessed. You've heard about Job's patience, and seen how it all played out with the Lord, how the Lord's loaded with compassion and mercy. But above everything, my brothers, don't swear — not by heaven, not by earth, not by no oath at all — just let your "yes" be "yes" and your "no" be "no," so you don't fall into some two-faced act.

web_text: Is any among you suffering? Let him pray. Is any cheerful? Let him sing praises. Is any among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the assembly, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord, and the prayer of faith will heal him who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. If he has committed sins, he will be forgiven. Confess your offenses to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The insistent prayer of a righteous person is powerfully effective.
slang_text: Anybody among you sufferin'? Let him pray. Anybody feelin' stoked? Let him sing praises. Anybody among you sick? Let him call for the church elders, and let 'em pray over him, anointin' him with oil in the Lord's name, and a faith-filled prayer's gonna heal the sick guy, and the Lord's gonna raise him back up. If he's sinned, he's gettin' forgiven. Confess your screwups to each other, pray for each other, so you can get healed. A righteous person's persistent prayer packs some serious power.

web_text: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain, and it didn’t rain on the earth for three years and six months. He prayed again, and the sky gave rain, and the earth produced its fruit. Brothers, if any among you wanders from the truth and someone turns him back, let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.
slang_text: Elijah was just a regular dude, same as us, and he prayed hard for it not to rain, and it didn't rain a drop for three whole years and six months. Then he prayed again, and the sky finally opened up, and the earth grew its crops. Brothers, if anybody among you wanders off from the truth and somebody brings him back, let that guy know: whoever turns a sinner back from his wrong path saves a soul from death and covers up a whole mountain of sins.
